local_validation.summary: The stack rebase exposed two current-kernel contract shifts: pg_connect now accepts PostgreSQL URLs rather than libpq key/value DSNs, and KernelHTTPRoute handlers receive typed KernelHTTPRequest values rather than legacy alists. The repair updates self-provisioned live proof scripts to inject postgresql:// URLs, adapts the mutation manifest body/path helpers to read typed request cells with alist fallback, and restores the public-front-door-flip boundary phrase in the router forms. Form live DB proofs, A/B observation, public gate harness, runtime surface report, focused pytest, ruff, index check, and whitespace check pass.

known_limitations.observed: No ordinary no-header mutation traffic moved. The harnesses still show no-header traffic fanning out to FastAPI.
The deployed X-Form-Native-Public-Gate canary remains the next boundary before any no-header traffic flip.
Only throwaway local Postgres fixtures were used. No production database writes were attempted.

ci_validation.notes: Pending force-push of the rebased root branch and GitHub checks on PR #2586.

deploy_validation.notes: No public deploy attempted in this repair. Production remains at the previously aligned main deploy until PR #2586 lands and deploy verification runs.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Header-gated native mutation preview and public-gate routes now run against the typed KernelHTTPRequest carrier after rebase, while no-header traffic remains fanout-python.
test_flows: cd api && python3 -m pytest -q focused native stack tests -> 84 passed
python3 scripts/runtime_surface_report.py --json -> 33 kernel-first capable routes and 0 live front-door served

phase_gate.reason: Local stack repair is proven; the next phase still requires deployed X-Form-Native-Public-Gate canary evidence before any ordinary no-header mutation flip.
